name: Code2Video
readme_summary: Code2Video is a Python-based framework that generates educational videos directly from code. It leverages a multi-agent approach to transform code into visual explanations, aiming to simplify the creation of learning content.
primary_use_case: Automated generation of educational videos from source code.
cover_image_prompt: A magical quill pen, labeled 'Code2Video', is actively writing lines of code onto a blank scroll. As the quill writes, the code magically transforms into a vibrant, animated educational video playing out on the scroll itself, illustrating concepts like algorithms or data structures. The background is a cozy, modern study with subtle digital particles flowing from the quill. Small, transparent UI elements showing code snippets and video playback controls are visible around the scroll. The overall mood is creative and educational, with warm, inviting lighting. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with clear, friendly visuals and a focus on the transformation from code to video.

name: adbpad
description: AdpPad is a GUI software to test android apps using adb. This software is running Windows and macOS.
readme_summary: AdbPad is a cross-platform GUI application for Windows and macOS that simplifies Android app testing by providing a user-friendly interface for executing ADB commands, managing connected devices, sending text input, capturing screenshots, and creating virtual displays.
primary_use_case: Streamlining Android app testing through a graphical user interface for ADB operations.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly robot assistant, labeled 'AdbPad', is holding a tablet displaying a vibrant Android interface. The robot is using a digital wand to seamlessly connect to multiple Android devices shown as floating orbs. One orb is being 'typed' on by the robot's wand, another is showing a captured screenshot, and a third is projecting a larger virtual display. Subtle lines of code and data streams flow between the robot and the devices, indicating command execution and data transfer. The scene is set in a clean, modern workshop with soft, focused lighting. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a bright and approachable color palette, emphasizing ease of use and efficient testing.

name: mcp-toolbox
description: MCP Toolbox for Databases is an open source MCP server for databases.
readme_summary: MCP Toolbox for Databases is an open-source Go-based server that bridges AI agents, IDEs, and applications with enterprise databases. It offers both a ready-to-use MCP server with prebuilt tools for immediate data interaction and a framework for building custom, secure AI tools with specific logic.
key_features: Connects AI agents, IDEs, and applications to enterprise databases
Prebuilt generic tools for instant data exploration (e.g., list_tables, execute_sql)
Custom tools framework for building specialized, secure AI tools
Provides SDKs for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Java
primary_use_case: To enable AI agents and applications to interact with and query enterprise databases through the Model Context Protocol (MCP), offering both out-of-the-box functionality and a platform for custom tool development.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, ancient librarian standing at a grand, ornate desk, holding a glowing digital tablet. The tablet displays a complex database schema, and with a gesture, the librarian conjures shimmering, organized books representing data. Around the desk, AI-like robotic assistants eagerly receive these books, while small, stylized icons of various database systems (like PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc.) are subtly integrated into the library's architecture. The scene is bathed in warm, inviting light, emphasizing knowledge and accessibility. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic, highlighting the transformation of raw data into understandable information for AI.

name: review-prompts
readme_summary: This repository provides AI-assisted code review prompts specifically tailored for Linux kernel, systemd, and iproute development. It integrates with AI tools like Claude Code and offers specialized commands for reviewing, debugging, and verifying code within these projects.
key_features: Specialized prompts for Linux kernel, systemd, and iproute
Integration with AI tools (e.g., Claude Code)
Slash commands for review, debug, and verify workflows
Context-aware skill loading based on working directory
Semcode integration for enhanced code navigation
primary_use_case: To enhance and automate the code review process for critical Linux-based system projects using AI.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian meticulously organizing and annotating ancient, complex scrolls representing code. The librarian uses a glowing magnifying glass to highlight specific sections, and as they do, small, intelligent digital sprites (representing AI) emerge from the scrolls, offering suggestions and corrections in a clear, organized manner. The scrolls are neatly arranged on shelves, with subtle UI elements and code snippets appearing as floating annotations around the librarian. The scene is a warm, inviting study filled with books and soft light, rendered in a detailed 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing clarity and order.

name: nanochat
description: The best ChatGPT that $100 can buy.
readme_summary: nanochat is a minimalist and hackable Python framework for training and experimenting with Large Language Models (LLMs) on a single GPU. It covers the full LLM lifecycle from tokenization to inference and includes a ChatGPT-like UI, enabling users to train models like GPT-2 for a fraction of the original cost.
key_features: Minimalist and hackable codebase for LLM training
Supports full LLM lifecycle: tokenization, pretraining, finetuning, evaluation, inference
Includes a ChatGPT-like web UI for interaction
Enables training GPT-2 capability models for under $100 on a single GPU node
Automated hyperparameter calculation for compute-optimal models
Time-to-GPT-2 leaderboard for tracking training speed and efficiency
Dependency management using uv for streamlined setup
primary_use_case: To provide an accessible and cost-effective platform for researchers and developers to train, experiment with, and deploy Large Language Models, particularly focusing on achieving GPT-2 level capabilities on minimal hardware.
cover_image_prompt: A miniature, highly detailed laboratory workbench where a single, powerful GPU glows with energy. From the GPU, a stream of digital 'building blocks' (representing data and code) flows into a complex, yet elegant, transformer model structure. This structure then elegantly transforms into a friendly, glowing chatbot avatar that is interacting with a user on a simple, clean screen. Subtle lines of code and data visualizations are etched into the workbench surface. The overall mood is one of focused innovation and accessibility, with a clean, bright aesthetic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear visual storytelling and a vibrant, tech-inspired color palette.

name: qui
description: A fast, single-binary qBittorrent web UI: manage multiple instances, automate torrent workflows, and cross-seed across trackers.
readme_summary: qui is a fast, single-binary web UI for qBittorrent, designed to manage multiple qBittorrent instances efficiently. It offers features like cross-seeding, automated torrent workflows, and backups, all accessible through a responsive interface.
primary_use_case: Managing multiple qBittorrent instances and automating torrent workflows from a single, centralized web interface.
cover_image_prompt: A conductor standing at a podium, orchestrating multiple diverse orchestras (representing qBittorrent instances) playing in harmony. Each orchestra has unique instruments and sheet music (representing different torrents and trackers). The conductor uses a glowing baton to seamlessly guide the flow of music, with streams of data flowing from one orchestra to another, symbolizing cross-seeding. Subtle digital interfaces and progress bars are visible on the podium. The scene is set in a grand, modern concert hall with a clean, organized aesthetic. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant colors and clear visual storytelling, emphasizing control and efficiency.
